title: PDF Generator using groups from board

posts.author: Davedvine (Dave Vineburg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi legends, is there a document generator app that allows you to pull groups from a board into multiple separate tables on a PDF/WordDoc?

Docugen doesn’t have the ability

Bonus points if it can also pull figures from a formula column

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml:
@Davedvine It so happens we have other customers who requested the same feature - you can vote for it too!

DocuGen Community – 3 Jun 22

Insert multiple tables from multiple board groups in the same document

Ideas & feature requests

Currently, when generating tables, you can only include data from one board group in a single document. Oftentimes, there is a need for more than one table per document. It would be ideal to have the option to include multiple tables from multiple...

Could you shed some light on what type of document this is? I would like to better understand the use case.

posts.author: Davedvine (Dave Vineburg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Construction company. We use one board per project. Groups are different aspects of the project that can be brought into the final proposal.

It’s like a price book we select what we want to bring into the proposal depending on the project.

Items are our client facing titles with sub items making up the material and labour.

A client may want to remove an aspect of the budget doesn’t allow so we group them as it is easier to read

IMG_14261170×1997 311 KB

IMG_14251170×1915 287 KB

IMG_14271170×1987 135 KB

posts.author: samicaracand (Sami Caracand (DocuGen))
posts.cookedHtml:
@Davedvine fantastic explanation – makes perfect sense; thank you!
I will add this to my team’s backlog for discussion. I can’t promise anything before mid-year as we’re deep into building our new e-signature feature for DocuGen.
